understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by positioning the selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, progressing through all levels until the game reports WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions shift the currently selected piece one grid step (in 3-pixel-per-cell coordinates) unless blocked by walls/boundaries; if a pushable block is directly adjacent in the movement direction, the piece can push it along as part of the move if space allows. Levels can contain multiple selectables, and CLICK switches which selectable is controlled by targeting its exact pixel/grid position as shown in the state. Walls act as immovable obstacles that prevent movement/pushing through them, though the blocked-direction indicator is only a hint and the actual result depends on the engine's collision rules.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear boxed in by walls, so test if the boundary wall allows any movement by attempting to move right.
history.reasoning: We appear boxed in by walls, so test if the boundary wall allows any movement by attempting to move right.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear enclosed by walls on all sides; try moving right to probe if any boundary allows sliding/overlap despite the blocked hint.
history.reasoning: We appear enclosed by walls on all sides; try moving right to probe if any boundary allows sliding/overlap despite the blocked hint.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear boxed in; try moving right into the adjacent selectable/wall to see if it pushes or swaps, which may be the only way to progress.
history.reasoning: We appear boxed in; try moving right into the adjacent selectable/wall to see if it pushes or swaps, which may be the only way to progress.

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | We’re boxed in by walls on all sides, so we must switch to the other selectable and maneuver it onto the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on all sides, so we must switch to the other selectable and maneuver it onto the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_RIGHT | Move onto the adjacent goal area to occupy it and trigger the win condition if goals only require one selectable.
history.reasoning: Move onto the adjacent goal area to occupy it and trigger the win condition if goals only require one selectable.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_RIGHT | We need the selected piece to occupy the goal tile; try moving onto the adjacent goal area to the right despite the blocked hint.
history.reasoning: We need the selected piece to occupy the goal tile; try moving onto the adjacent goal area to the right despite the blocked hint.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_LEFT | We’re adjacent to the goal area; try moving left onto the goal tile (walls may still allow entering the non-collidable goal).
history.reasoning: We’re adjacent to the goal area; try moving left onto the goal tile (walls may still allow entering the non-collidable goal).

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_UP | Move onto the central goal tile; goals are non-collidable so this should be allowed despite the blocked hint.
history.reasoning: Move onto the central goal tile; goals are non-collidable so this should be allowed despite the blocked hint.
